title: pyrus malus subsp. paradisiaca
intro: Pyrus malus subsp. paradisiaca, commonly known as the paradise apple or pomegranate-grafted apple, is a variety of the domesticated apple tree (Malus domestica) that has been grafted with the pomegranate tree (Punica granatum). The paradise apple is valued for its unique combination of the health benefits of both apples and pomegranates, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce cholesterol levels, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the paradise apple include teas, tinctures, and juices, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
